children.heading: Records
children.label: a Records
children.text: Each State and local government submitting any application for a plan which is approved under , and each public utility and person or persons entering into a contract under such a plan, shall keep such records and make such reports as the Secretary may require. The Secretary and the Comptroller General of the United States shall have access, at reasonable times and under reasonable conditions, to any books, documents, papers, records, and reports of each such State and local government, utility, and person or persons which the Secretary determines, in consultation with the Comptroller General of the United States, are pertinent to this part.section 8235a(a) of this title
an estimate of the total amount of energy saved as a result of the activities carried out under this part;
an estimate of the annual savings in energy anticipated as a result of each prototype residential energy efficiency program established under a plan approved under ;section 8235a(a) of this title

an analysis, developed in consultation with the Federal Trade Commission and the Department of Justice, of the impact on competition of each prototype residential energy efficiency program established under a plan approved under ; andsection 8235a(a) of this title

if the Secretary determines that it is appropriate, an analysis of the impact of expanding the approval of plans under to establish prototype residential energy efficiency programs, and the provision of financial assistance to such programs, on a national basis and an assessment of the alternative methods by which such an expansion could be accomplished.section 8235a(a) of this title
